rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERNESTO MARTIN JAUREGUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Fees To Conservator And Conservators Attorneys And Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the conservator's hourly rate appears to be $175, not $145, as stated in the petition and no new hourly proposed hourly fee schedule appears to have been filed. [Probate Code Section 2614.8.] It would also have been helpful, had the petition addressed the nature of some of the services provided by the conservator of the person in slightly more detail, as prior fee petitions had.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT D. HARMON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/24, in part because of the recently filed objection on 10/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Other issue(s) necessitating continuance: The original bank statement has not yet been filed. Please provide a courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/6/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Cash is cash, regardless of which bank holds it; there is no need to specify out of which specific bank account cash is to be distributed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The proposed order will be modified to clarify that: (1) Payments remitted by the petitioner from 04/06/2023 through 07/29/2024 in the total amount of $30,887.60 to JMBM for legal services rendered from 03/13/2023 through 06/30/2024 are approved; (2) Additional retainer payment remitted by the petitioner on or about 11/15/2023 to JMBM in the amount of $10,000 is approved; (3) Payments to JMBM from its client retainer in the total amount of $2,041.50 for legal services rendered from 07/01/2024 through 08/31/2024 are approved; and (4) JMBM is authorized to charge the estate fees and costs up to $17,958.50, payment and reimbursement of which may be made without further court order from the current retainer, subject to court review at the time of the petitioner's accounting.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OTIS R. DAMSLET
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024 and subsequently posted as tentative rulings for previous 2 hearing), since this petition was initially set for 07/10/2024 and has been continued 3 times: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be amended and renoticed to pray for a specific period (e.g., months) for which the administration of this estate is sought to be continued. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2). 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) Each status report must include all allegations required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) ("allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution"), even if they may have been included in previous reports. 6) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account covering the 2-year period from the date of death has been filed. See LRSF 14.34(A). If the beneficiaries waived the first account, please file signed waivers. Otherwise, the Court may require the first account. See section 12201(c)(1). Note: The status report cannot be amended to add the first account. The first account must be filed as a new accounting petition with a higher filing fee. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND C. LOW AND IRENE C. LOW REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 6, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner?S Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents From Respondent Lydia Lee And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Lydia M. Villarreal. Before the Court is Petitioner Joseph Wu ("Propounding Party" or "Petitioner")'s Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production, Set One, from Respondent Lydia Lee and For Monetary Sanctions (the "Motion"). The Motion is GRANTED in part, for the reasons set forth below. Respondent Lydia Lee ("Responding Party" or "Respondent") served Respondent Lydia Lee's Amended Responses to Petitioner Joseph Wu's Requests for Production, Set One, which included amended responses to all but one Request for Production ("RFPs") on October 17, 2024 (the "Amended Responses"). (See Exhibit I to Declaration of Steven A. Ellenberg in Support of Opposition to Petitioner's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production, Set One, and For Monetary Sanctions). Responding Party did not amend her response to RFP No. 48. Since Responding Party has served amended responses, Petitioner's Motion is moot as to RFP Nos. 1 through 47 and 49 through 80. Petitioner's Motion is GRANTED as to RFP No. 48. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.310, on receipt of a response to a demand for production, the propounding party may move for an order compelling further response to the demand if the propounding party determines that the statement of compliance with the demand is incomplete. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.220, a statement of compliance shall state that the production, inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, and related activity demanded, will be allowed either in whole or in part, and that all documents or things in the demanded category that are in the possession, custody, or control of that party and to which no objection is being made will be included in the production. This Court finds Responding Party's statement of compliance in response to RFP No. 48 inadequate. Responding Party shall serve a further response to RFP No. 48 in conformity with Section 2031.220 within 15 days from the issuance of this Order. Petitioner's request for an Order compelling the production of a privilege log is GRANTED. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.240, if an objection is based on a claim of privilege or work product doctrine, the response shall provide sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim, including a privilege log if necessary. This Court acknowledges that Responding Party asserted the attorney-client privilege and or work -product doctrine for many of Respondent's responses to Petitioner's RFPs. Responding Party shall produce a privilege log within 30 days from the issuance of this Order. Propounding Party's request that this Court issue an order compelling further amended responses is DENIED without prejudice. Responding Party's Amended Responses are not properly before the Court for the following reasons: (1) an Order compelling further responses as to the Amended Responses would be premature because Propounding Party has 45 days from October 17, 2024, to meet and confer concerning the amended responses (See Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.310, subd. (c)); (2) Propounding Party must first attempt to meet and confer with Responding Party concerning the Amended Responses before Propounding Party can bring a motion to compel on the Amended Responses (See Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.310, subd. (b)(2)); and (3) Propounding Party has not provided a Separate Statement as to the Amended Responses to identify how the Amended Responses are deficient (See Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND C. LOW AND IRENE C. LOW REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 6, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, From Respondent Lydia Lee And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: (part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Lydia M. Villarreal. Before the Court is Petitioner Joseph Wu ("Propounding Party" or "Petitioner")'s Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, from Respondent Lydia Lee and For Monetary Sanctions (the "Motion"). The Motion is GRANTED in part, for the reasons set forth below. Respondent Lydia Lee ("Responding Party" or "Respondent") served Respondent Lydia Lee's Amended Responses to Petitioner Joseph Wu's Form Interrogatories-General, Set One, which included amended responses to all but two Form Interrogatories ("FROGs") on October 17, 2024 (the "Amended Responses"). (See Exhibit D to Declaration of Steven A. Ellenberg in Support of Opposition to Petitioner's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, and For Monetary Sanctions). Responding Party did not amend her responses to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4. Since Responding Party has served amended responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One), Petitioner's Motion is moot as to FROG Nos. 1.1, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 4.1, 4.2, 9.1, 9.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 14.2, 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5, and 50.6. Petitioner's Motion is GRANTED as to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4. The propounding party may move for an order compelling further response if the propounding party determines that an objection to an interrogatory is without merit or too general. Cal. Code Civil Proc. sec. 2030.300, subd. (a)(3). Responding Party failed to substantively respond to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4 and failed to subsequently amend those responses in her Amended Responses. As such, Petitioner's Motion is GRANTED as to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND C. LOW AND IRENE C. LOW REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 6, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petitioner?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, From Respondent Lydia Lee And For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) Petitioner's request for sanctions in the amount of $7,425.00 is GRANTED. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. secs. 2023.010 and 2023.020, failing to meet and confer in good faith is a sanctionable misuse of the discovery process. Responding Party ignored Propounding Party's efforts to meet and confer and failed to return Propounding Party's telephone calls and voicemail messages. (See Petitioner's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, From Respondent Lydia Lee and for Monetary Sanctions at p. 3:3-8). Propounding Party brought the instant Motion as a direct consequence of Responding Party's abject failure to meet and confer in good faith, which constitutes a sanctionable misuse of the discovery process pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. secs. 2023.010(i) and 2023.020. Furthermore, although Responding Party served Amended Responses after the instant Motion was filed, Propounding Party is still subject to sanctions pursuant to Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1348. Therefore, Propounding Party's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Respondent Lydia Lee and her counsel Steven A. Ellenberg of Lathrop GPM LLP, jointly and severally, are ordered to pay Petitioner Joseph Wu seven thousand four hundred and twenty-five dollars within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. Furthermore, Responding Party is ORDERED to meet and confer in good faith with Propounding Party concerning FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4. Responding Party shall initiate the meet and confer process within 5 days from the issuance of this Order and the parties must complete the meet and confer process within 10 days from the issuance of this Order. The parties are ORDERED to conduct the meet and confer process either telephonically, by Zoom, or in-person. Responding Party shall serve further responses to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4 within 30 days of the issuance of this Order. Responding Party's responses to FROG Nos. 2.3 and 2.4 shall comply with Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2030.210 et seq. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND C. LOW AND IRENE C. LOW REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 6, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petitioner?S Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents From Respondent Lydia Lee And For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 2) Similarly, Petitioner's request for an Order compelling production of documents is DENIED without prejudice. The only document Responding Party indicated she would produce in her initial responses served on July 31, 2024, in response to RFP No. 48, is the promissory note at issue in this matter. Aside from RFP No. 48, Responding Party did not put forth a single substantive response to indicate whether she would produce other relevant documents in her possession, custody or control. To the extent Propounding Party seeks an Order compelling compliance with the production of documents Responding Party indicated she had in her possession, custody or control in her Amended Responses, those responses are not currently before the Court. See Cal. Code of Civil Proc. sec. 2031.320. Petitioner's request for sanctions in the amount of $7,412.00 is GRANTED. Pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. secs. 2023.010 and 2023.020, failing to meet and confer in good faith is a sanctionable misuse of the discovery process. Responding Party ignored Propounding Party's efforts to meet and confer and failed to return Propounding Party's telephone calls and voicemail messages. (See Petitioner's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production, Set One, From Respondent Lydia Lee and for Monetary Sanctions at p. 2:24 - p.3:5). Propounding Party brought the instant Motion as a direct consequence of Responding Party's abject failure to meet and confer in good faith, which constitutes a sanctionable misuse of the discovery process pursuant to Cal. Code of Civil Proc. secs. 2023.010(i) and 2023.020. Furthermore, although Responding Party served Amended Responses after the instant Motion was filed, Propounding Party is still subject to sanctions pursuant to Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1348. Therefore, Propounding Party's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Respondent Lydia Lee and her counsel Steven A. Ellenberg of Lathrop GPM LLP, jointly and severally, are ordered to pay Petitioner Joseph Wu seven thousand four hundred and twelve dollars within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PHOEBE CLAIRE DE CASTRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Directing Disposition Of Minor'S Funds
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Notice of Hearing is not on file. Proof of service was filed indicating Notice of Hearing was served; however, the use of Judicial Council form DE-120 is mandatory. (2) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. 6) (3) Petition may need to clarify a projective closing time of the UTMA account - until the minor attains the age of 18 or older. 4) Petition conclusively alleges that a guardianship proceeding would be unnecessary, expensive, and an unwise use of time and resources for the estate of the minor. A guardian of the estate could still invest the funds for the best interest of the minor under the court's supervision if the court does not favor transfers to a UTMA account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOYCE DOWNS REVOCABLE INTER-VIVOS TRUST DATED JUNE 8, 2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) This does not appear to be the proper venue; the trustee resides in Los Angeles. (3) The wrong form of notice was served. (4) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) Who are all of the heirs of the grantor? They must be identified and must be personally served notice if they are not trust beneficiaries. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (6) If the petition is not dismissed, please file a copy of the vesting deed to show how title to the real property is currently held. Deadline is 12/6/24for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., so the petition was only cursorily reviewed online.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FRANCIS BRIAN REILLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No procedural issue to clear. The court to determine. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
